articles.title: US and China both believe the other is a declining power
articles.summary: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.

articles.title: Cheap Drones Remain Wild Card in Iran war
articles.summary: Stopping Iran’s production of drones is critical to opening the Strait of Hormuz and halting its attacks on Gulf nations. But can it be done?

articles.title: An Increase in U.S. Troops to the Middle East, and a String of Attacks on Jewish Sites
articles.summary: Plus, a jaw-dropping A.I. tool goes dark.

articles.title: Ukraine Finally Got Battlefield Momentum. Now Comes a Russian Offensive.
articles.summary: Moscow’s forces are intensifying their attacks in southern Ukraine after Kyiv made rare gains along the front.

articles.title: Pentagon orders troops from 82nd Airborne Division to deploy to Middle East
articles.summary: Nearly a month into the war with Iran, the Trump administration is keeping its options open: it says it is pursuing diplomatic solutions with Iran, while ordering thousands of paratroopers to deploy i

articles.title: They gave her business a lifeline, then froze all her money
articles.summary: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.

articles.title: The 7 Biggest Decluttering Myths
articles.summary: Experts said these were the most commonly held beliefs about organization — and they’re holding you back.

articles.title: An American in Russia Is Linked to Neo-Nazi Terror Cells Across Europe
articles.summary: F.B.I. agents thought they had weakened an online hate group known as the Base. A string of European terrorism cases indicates it has resurged.

articles.title: Your data is everywhere. The government is buying it without a warrant
articles.summary: Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.

articles.title: Are you sure you know what 'gaslighting' is?
articles.summary: Therapists say we're overusing the word. Here's what it actually means — and what the Ingrid Bergman film that helped birth the word can teach us about it.

articles.title: How Trump's Iran war objectives have shifted over time
articles.summary: Here is a reminder of some of what he has said - and where the US is now.

articles.title: A shelter village provides a bridge to permanent housing
articles.summary: Shelter villages offer temporary and private places for the unhoused to sleep and store belongings. One of the newest, The Bridge, opened recently in central Illinois.

articles.title: Islandwide blackouts bring life in Cuba to a near total halt
articles.summary: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.

articles.title: Democrat Emily Gregory Wins Florida Special Election in Mar-a-Lago’s District
articles.summary: Emily Gregory’s victory in Palm Beach brought the Democratic surge to President Trump’s backyard, while a union leader leads in a race for a state senate seat vacated by Florida’s lieutenant governor.

articles.title: OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns
articles.summary: OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

articles.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

articles.title: Air Canada Pilots Killed in LaGuardia Crash Were Early in Flying Careers
articles.summary: The pilots were identified as Antoine Forest, 30, and Mackenzie Gunther. They were the only two fatalities in the plane’s collision with a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.

articles.title: A Day in the Life of a New York City Junklugger
articles.summary: The work force behind Junkluggers, a company that tosses, donates and resells what we leave behind, bears witness to grief and lives in transition.

articles.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
articles.summary: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

articles.title: Will President Trump act on his threat to take Cuba?
articles.summary: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.

articles.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
articles.summary: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

articles.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
articles.summary: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
